document_name: 1997 Documentation Guidelines for Evaluation and Management Services
note: Primary documentation framework for history, examination, and MDM; useful as fallback and for non-99202-99215 documentation rules.

source_references.document: 1997 Documentation Guidelines for Evaluation and Management Services
source_references.official_title: Documentation Guidelines for Evaluation and Management Services
key_sections: General Principles of Medical Record Documentation
Documentation of the Complexity of Medical Decision Making

general_principles.source_section: General Principles of Medical Record Documentation
principles: The medical record should be complete and legible.

Each encounter should document the reason for the encounter, relevant history, examination findings, prior diagnostic test results, assessment/clinical impression/diagnosis, plan of care, date, and the observer's identity.
If it is not explicitly documented, the reason for ordering diagnostic or ancillary services should be inferable from the record.
Past and present diagnoses should be accessible to the treating or consulting physician.
Appropriate health risk factors should be identified.
The patient's progress, response to treatment, and revision of diagnosis should be documented.
Reported CPT and ICD-9-CM codes must be supported by the medical record.

history_types_table.rule_text: A history type is valid only when the required CC, HPI, ROS, and PFSH elements for that level are met; chief complaint is required at all levels.

chief_complaint.exact_definition: A concise statement of the reason for the encounter, usually in the patient's own words.
chief_complaint.documentation_guideline: The medical record should clearly reflect the chief complaint.

hpi.exact_definition: A chronological description of the development of the present illness from first sign or symptom to the present.

extended.definition: At least four HPI elements, or the status of at least three chronic or inactive conditions.

hpi.source_section: Documentation of History - History of Present Illness (HPI)

ros.exact_definition: An inventory of body systems obtained through questions about signs or symptoms the patient may be experiencing.

problem_pertinent.definition: Questions about the system directly related to the HPI problem.
problem_pertinent.documentation_guideline: Document positive responses and pertinent negatives for the related system.

extended.definition: Related system plus a limited number of additional systems.
extended.documentation_guideline: Document positive responses and pertinent negatives for 2 to 9 systems.

complete.definition: Related system plus all additional body systems.
complete.documentation_guideline: Document at least 10 systems; remaining systems may be recorded as negative if clearly stated.

ros.source_section: Documentation of History - Review of Systems (ROS)

pfsh.exact_definition: A review of past history, family history, and social history.

components.past_history: Past illnesses, operations, injuries, and treatments.
components.family_history: Medical events in the patient's family, including hereditary disease or risk factors.
components.social_history: Age-appropriate review of past and current activities.

pertinent.definition: History area(s) directly related to the HPI problem.
pertinent.documentation_guideline: At least one specific item from any one of the three areas.

complete_established.definition: Complete PFSH for established patient and certain other categories.
complete_established.documentation_guideline: At least one specific item from two of the three areas.

complete_new.definition: Complete PFSH for new patient and certain comprehensive services.
complete_new.documentation_guideline: At least one specific item from all three areas.

pfsh.source_section: Documentation of History - Past, Family and/or Social History (PFSH)

detailed.requirement: At least 2 bullet elements from each of 6 areas/systems, or at least 12 bullet elements across 2 or more areas/systems

comprehensive.requirement: At least 9 areas/systems; perform all bullet elements in each selected area/system and document at least 2 elements per area

detailed.requirement: At least 12 bullet elements for most systems; at least 9 for eye and psychiatric examinations

comprehensive.requirement: All bullet elements; every element in shaded boxes and at least one element in each unshaded box

mdm_framework.source_section: Documentation of the Complexity of Medical Decision Making
mdm_framework.rule_text: Two of the three MDM elements must be met or exceeded.
elements: number of diagnoses or management options
amount and/or complexity of data reviewed
risk of significant complications, morbidity, and/or mortality
mdm_framework.note: This is the CMS 1997 MDM framework and is useful as a fallback documentation model.

risk_of_significant_complications_morbid.source_section: Risk of Significant Complications, Morbidity, and/or Mortality
risk_of_significant_complications_morbid.note: See the Table of Risk on page 47 for the detailed risk categories.

counseling_or_coordination_of_care.source_section: Documentation of an Encounter Dominated by Counseling or Coordination of Care
counseling_or_coordination_of_care.note: When counseling or coordination of care dominates the encounter, time becomes the controlling factor.
